meta.name: DAST-10 Drug Abuse Screening Test
meta.description: The DAST-10 is a brief, 10-item self-report screening instrument for identifying individuals who are abusing psychoactive drugs. It yields a quantitative index of the degree of consequences related to drug abuse.
meta.author: Skinner (1982)
meta.reference: Skinner HA. The Drug Abuse Screening Test. Addict Behav. 1982;7(4):363-371.
meta.instruction: The following questions concern information about your potential involvement with drugs, excluding alcohol and tobacco, during the past 12 months. Please answer Yes or No.

questions.text: Have you used drugs other than those required for medical reasons?
Do you abuse more than one drug at a time?
Are you always able to stop using drugs when you want to?
Have you ever had blackouts or flashbacks as a result of drug use?
Do you ever feel bad or guilty about your drug use?
Does your spouse (or parents) ever complain about your involvement with drugs?
Have you neglected your family because of your use of drugs?
Have you engaged in illegal activities in order to obtain drugs?
Have you ever experienced withdrawal symptoms (felt sick) when you stopped taking drugs?
Have you had medical problems as a result of your drug use (e.g., memory loss, hepatitis, convulsions, bleeding)?

ranges.label: No Problems Reported
ranges.description: Your DAST-10 score indicates no drug-related problems over the past 12 months. Your responses suggest that you are not currently involved in problematic drug use and are maintaining a healthy, substance-free lifestyle.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current drug-free or responsible lifestyle choices
Stay informed about the health risks and legal consequences of recreational drug use
Be a supportive presence for friends or family members who may be struggling with substance use

ranges.label: Low Level
ranges.description: Your score suggests a low level of drug-related problems. While not currently severe, these early indicators show that drug use may be beginning to affect certain areas of your life. Continued self-monitoring and awareness are advisable.
suggestions: Pay close attention to your drug use patterns and honestly assess whether they are increasing over time
Consider discussing your substance use openly with a healthcare provider at your next visit
Develop and practice healthy coping strategies for stress, boredom, and emotional difficulties
Educate yourself about the risks of escalation from occasional to regular drug use

ranges.label: Moderate Level
ranges.description: Your score indicates a moderate level of drug-related problems that may be affecting your health, relationships, or daily responsibilities. A professional evaluation is recommended to assess whether a substance use disorder may be developing.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a healthcare provider or addiction specialist for a comprehensive substance use assessment
Explore counseling or therapy options that specialize in substance use, such as cognitive behavioral therapy
Identify the specific triggers, situations, and emotional states that lead you to use drugs and develop alternative responses
Talk to someone you trust about your concerns, as social support is a key factor in successful behavior change
Consider attending a support group to connect with others who understand what you are going through

ranges.label: Substantial Level
ranges.description: Your score suggests a substantial level of drug-related problems consistent with a possible substance use disorder. Drug use at this level is likely causing significant harm to your physical health, mental well-being, and daily functioning.
suggestions: Seek professional treatment for substance use as soon as possible from an addiction medicine specialist or treatment center
Consider enrolling in an intensive outpatient program or residential treatment program depending on your situation
Inform trusted family members or close friends about your situation so they can provide accountability and support
Avoid people, places, and situations that trigger or facilitate your drug use

ranges.label: Severe Level
ranges.description: Your score indicates a severe level of drug-related problems strongly suggestive of drug dependence. Immediate professional intervention is essential, as continued use at this level poses serious risks to your health, safety, and overall well-being.
suggestions: Seek immediate professional help from an addiction treatment center, hospital emergency department, or your primary care provider
Do not attempt to stop using drugs suddenly on your own, as withdrawal from certain substances can be medically dangerous without supervision
Contact the SAMHSA National Helpline at 1-800-662-4357 for free confidential treatment referrals available 24/7
Let trusted family members or friends know about your situation so they can help you access and stay in treatment
Remember that substance use disorders are medical conditions and recovery is possible with proper professional support
